title: tri party repo / repo market / financial infrastructure | Regulatory Guidance | Statement on the Release of the Tri-party Repo Infrastructur...
summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York issued a statement on February 15, 2012 responding to the Tri-party Repo Infrastructure Reform Task Force's final report, acknowledging progress in operational changes but noting that systemic risk in the tri-party repo market remained unchanged because reliance on intraday credit from clearing banks had not been meaningfully reduced. The Fed announced it would intensify direct oversight of clearing banks and FICC and explore additional policy options including collateral restrictions and an industry-financed liquidation facility.
